meta.title: DBX-DEP-6: Full-Stack Engineering, Governance and Cost Optimization

questions.stem: A team is standardizing production deployments for Python ETL code across workspaces. Which approach BEST supports repeatable CI/CD on Databricks?

options.text: Package code in version control and deploy jobs/resources with Databricks Asset Bundles
options.explanation: Bundles pair declarative resource configuration with version-controlled source so the same project can be validated and promoted across environments. The other options increase manual drift.

options.text: Keep business logic in personal notebooks and copy them manually
Install libraries interactively on a long-running cluster
Export notebooks as HTML and upload them during releases

questions.stem: A Lakeflow pipeline must use a small reusable transform in several tables and flows. What design choice is MOST maintainable?

options.text: Implement the transform as a reusable function and call it from pipeline code
options.explanation: Reusable functions reduce duplication and are easier to test in CI. Copy-paste logic creates inconsistency and dashboards are not a code reuse mechanism.

options.text: Duplicate the transformation in each table definition
Convert the transform into a dashboard tile
Run the transform only in an analyst notebook before each release

questions.stem: A team receives JSON files continuously in cloud storage and needs incremental discovery with schema inference. Which Databricks feature is the BEST fit?

options.text: Auto Loader using the cloudFiles source
options.explanation: Auto Loader is designed for incremental file discovery and scalable ingestion from cloud object storage. The other options do not provide managed incremental file ingestion.

options.text: A deep clone of the landing directory
A static batch read of all files every run
A SQL view directly over the object store path

questions.stem: A streaming source occasionally delivers duplicate events after retries. Which pattern BEST improves idempotent bronze ingestion?

options.text: Write to Delta with a checkpoint and deduplicate using a business key plus event time strategy
options.explanation: Checkpointing preserves streaming progress, and deduplication with stable keys/event-time logic addresses replayed records. VACUUM and CSV do not solve duplicate event processing.

options.text: Turn off checkpointing so duplicates are easier to see
Append to CSV so files can be edited later
Use VACUUM after each micro-batch

questions.stem: A silver-layer pipeline must enforce data quality in Lakeflow Declarative Pipelines. Select TWO statements that are correct. (Select TWO)

options.text: Expectations can warn, drop invalid records, or fail processing depending on configuration
options.explanation: Lakeflow expectations are designed to express quality rules within the pipeline and can warn, drop, or fail. They do not get replaced by auto optimization and are not limited to SQL warehouses.

options.text: Expectation logic belongs close to the transformation where data quality is being asserted
options.explanation: Lakeflow expectations are designed to express quality rules within the pipeline and can warn, drop, or fail. They do not get replaced by auto optimization and are not limited to SQL warehouses.

options.text: AUTO OPTIMIZE automatically replaces expectations for bad-record handling
Expectations are supported only for batch SQL warehouses, not managed pipelines

questions.stem: A downstream gold table needs the latest customer attributes from a CDC stream. Which approach BEST fits this requirement?

options.text: Use MERGE or APPLY CHANGES semantics keyed by customer ID so updates replace older values
options.explanation: Latest-state targets require keyed upsert logic such as MERGE or managed CDC patterns. VACUUM removes obsolete files; it does not reconcile business state.

options.text: Append every change and let BI users figure out the latest row
Use VACUUM to collapse multiple versions into one row
Convert the target to Parquet so updates become faster

questions.stem: A provider wants to share curated Delta tables with another organization without copying data files into the consumer account. Which capability is MOST appropriate?

options.text: Delta Sharing
options.explanation: Delta Sharing is built for secure cross-organization sharing without replicating data files into a separate platform account. The alternatives either copy data or create brittle access patterns.

options.text: Deep clone
Mounting the provider storage account into the consumer workspace
Exporting the tables to CSV nightly

questions.stem: Analysts need to query external PostgreSQL tables from Databricks while keeping the source system authoritative. What pattern BEST matches this need?

options.text: Use Lakehouse Federation to query the operational data source without first copying all tables into Delta
options.explanation: Lakehouse Federation is meant for governed access to external database systems while leaving the source authoritative. CSV exports and catalog volumes do not provide the same federated query pattern.

options.text: Schedule an hourly CSV export and query the files
Use OPTIMIZE on the PostgreSQL source
Create a Unity Catalog volume for PostgreSQL system catalogs

questions.stem: Which artifact is MOST useful for restarting a Structured Streaming query without reprocessing already committed micro-batches?

options.text: The checkpoint directory
options.explanation: Structured Streaming stores offsets and state in the checkpoint location so the query can resume consistently. Table history and notifications are not enough to restore streaming progress.

options.text: The cluster event log
The Delta table history alone
The job email notification settings

questions.stem: A pipeline owner wants to inspect whether a streaming query is falling behind and how many input rows recent batches handled. Which interface is MOST directly relevant?

options.text: Streaming query progress metrics such as lastProgress
options.explanation: Streaming progress metadata includes per-batch input rows, durations, and state, which are central to diagnosing lag. The other artifacts do not expose streaming runtime progress.

options.text: VACUUM history
Unity Catalog tags
A Delta clone comparison

questions.stem: A Delta table suffers from many tiny files after frequent micro-batch writes. Which maintenance action BEST addresses this issue?

options.text: Run OPTIMIZE to compact small files
options.explanation: OPTIMIZE compacts small files into fewer larger files for better read performance. VACUUM removes obsolete files but does not compact active data.

options.text: Run VACUUM to rewrite files into larger ones
Disable checkpoints
Convert the table to JSON

questions.stem: A frequently filtered fact table is queried on several dimensions with evolving access patterns. Which layout choice is MOST consistent with current Databricks guidance when avoiding over-partitioning?

options.text: Use liquid clustering or Z-order style data skipping strategies instead of excessive static partitions
options.explanation: Databricks increasingly recommends avoiding overly granular partitions and using modern clustering/data-skipping techniques where appropriate. Per-customer partitioning is typically too fine-grained.

options.text: Create a partition for every distinct customer ID
Cache the table permanently in notebook memory for all users
Disable Photon so file skipping becomes deterministic

questions.stem: A team needs a governed, cloud-native way for Unity Catalog to access S3 or ADLS paths for external tables. Which object is required?

options.text: A storage credential attached to an external location
options.explanation: Unity Catalog uses storage credentials and external locations to govern external cloud storage access. Notebook permissions and dashboards do not provide storage authentication.

options.text: A workspace notebook permission
A cluster-scoped init script
A SQL dashboard subscription

questions.stem: A security team is implementing least-privilege data access in Unity Catalog. Select TWO accurate statements. (Select TWO)

options.text: Privileges are granted on securable objects such as catalogs, schemas, and tables
options.explanation: Unity Catalog access control is based on privileges over securable objects, and inheritance can reduce repetitive grants. Ownership is not required for simple read access, and cluster policies are separate from data permissions.

options.text: Privilege inheritance allows access management to be simplified at higher levels of the hierarchy
options.explanation: Unity Catalog access control is based on privileges over securable objects, and inheritance can reduce repetitive grants. Ownership is not required for simple read access, and cluster policies are separate from data permissions.

options.text: Users must be table owners to query any table
GRANT statements are replaced by cluster policies inside Unity Catalog

questions.stem: Which Unity Catalog capability MOST directly helps an engineer trace downstream tables impacted by a changed upstream dataset?

options.text: Lineage
options.explanation: Lineage captures upstream and downstream relationships across datasets and workloads. The other features are unrelated to dependency tracing.

options.text: VACUUM retention
Cluster autoscaling
Notebook widgets

questions.stem: A job runs successfully in development but fails in production because a required Python package is missing. What is the BEST long-term fix?

options.text: Declare the dependency in the job or bundle configuration so the runtime is recreated consistently
options.explanation: Explicitly managed dependencies support reproducible deployments. Manual installation and copied code create configuration drift.

options.text: Install the package manually on the production cluster after each failure
Paste the library source into the notebook
Lower the driver node size

questions.stem: A team wants to validate DataFrame transformation results automatically in CI before deploying. Which practice aligns BEST with Databricks testing guidance?

options.text: Use DataFrame and schema assertion helpers in automated tests around reusable transformation functions
options.explanation: Databricks documents DataFrame/schema assertions and encourages modular code that can be tested automatically. Visual review and unrelated maintenance operations are not a substitute for CI tests.

options.text: Rely only on visual notebook output review
Test only after the monthly production deployment
Replace unit tests with OPTIMIZE jobs

questions.stem: In a medallion architecture, which layer should MOST often contain standardized, deduplicated, conformed business entities ready for downstream reuse?

options.text: Silver
options.explanation: Silver typically contains cleaned, conformed, and deduplicated data that downstream gold outputs can build from. Bronze keeps raw source fidelity.

options.text: Bronze
Landing
Artifact

questions.stem: A gold dashboard requires pre-aggregated sales by region and month. Which design is MOST appropriate?

options.text: Store a curated business-level aggregate in the gold layer
options.explanation: Gold tables are intended for business-ready aggregations and consumption-oriented models. Bronze is not the right place for curated semantic outputs.

options.text: Keep only raw bronze files and aggregate in every dashboard query
Write dashboard extracts back into bronze
Use VACUUM to compute monthly totals

questions.stem: A new Lakeflow design includes conditional logic and iteration based on metadata tables. Select TWO statements that BEST match current Databricks guidance. (Select TWO)

options.text: Control-flow operators such as if/else can be part of pipeline logic
options.explanation: The professional blueprint includes conditional/control-flow patterns for production pipelines. Delta Sharing is unrelated, and pipelines are not limited to purely linear logic.

options.text: Metadata-driven branching can help one project deploy reusable patterns across datasets
options.explanation: The professional blueprint includes conditional/control-flow patterns for production pipelines. Delta Sharing is unrelated, and pipelines are not limited to purely linear logic.

options.text: Pipelines support only straight-line SQL with no branching
Control flow requires replacing Lakeflow with Delta Sharing
